dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The bell rings, signaling the end of another school day. For most, it's a release. For Shiwoon Yi, it's often when the real torment begins.

dialogue.speaker: Jang-ho
dialogue.line: Hey, Shiwoon. Where do you think you're going?

dialogue.speaker: Shiwoon Yi
dialogue.line: (thought) Not again...

dialogue.speaker: Min-woo
dialogue.line: Did you forget something, huh? Like, maybe... our lunch money?

dialogue.speaker: Shiwoon Yi
dialogue.line: I... I already gave it to you guys this morning.

dialogue.speaker: Jang-ho
dialogue.line: Oh? And you think that's enough? You think your pathetic allowance covers our 'protection fee' for the entire day?

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: THUD

dialogue.speaker: Shiwoon Yi
dialogue.line: (thought) My back... it hurts. Why can't I ever just fight back? Why am I so weak?

dialogue.speaker: Jang-ho
dialogue.line: Look at him, guys. He's about to cry. What a baby.

dialogue.speaker: Jun-seo
dialogue.line: Just give us what you have, Shiwoon. Or do you want another 'lesson'?

dialogue.speaker: Shiwoon Yi
dialogue.line: (thought) I hate this. I hate myself for enduring it.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: CRUMPLER

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Shiwoon hands over the crumpled bills, his eyes fixed on the ground. Another day, another humiliation.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The next morning, a new face graced the faculty lounge.

dialogue.speaker: Principal
dialogue.line: Everyone, I'd like to introduce our new English teacher, Mr. Chun-woo Han.

dialogue.speaker: Chun-woo Han
dialogue.line: (smirking) 'Sup. Call me Han. Or just 'teacher'. Whatever floats your boat.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The teachers exchanged uneasy glances. He was dressed casually, hair a bit wild, and carried an air of nonchalant rebellion.

dialogue.speaker: Shiwoon Yi
dialogue.line: (thought) He doesn't look like a teacher at all. He looks... like he just rolled out of bed.

dialogue.speaker: Chun-woo Han
dialogue.line: Alright, class. English is a language. Languages are tools. Tools are for getting what you want. So, what do you want?

dialogue.speaker: Student 1
dialogue.line: Uh... good grades?

dialogue.speaker: Chun-woo Han
dialogue.line: Boring! Think bigger. Power? Money? Revenge? English can get you all that. If you know how to use it.

dialogue.speaker: Shiwoon Yi
dialogue.line: (thought) Revenge? What kind of teacher talks about revenge?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Later that day, Shiwoon was heading home, trying to avoid the usual routes. He took a shortcut through a narrow alley.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: CLANG

dialogue.speaker: Thug 1
dialogue.line: Hey, old man! Hand over your wallet!

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Shiwoon froze. Up ahead, three burly men had cornered... Mr. Han?

dialogue.speaker: Chun-woo Han
dialogue.line: (sighs) Can't a man enjoy his afternoon stroll without being bothered by mosquitoes?

dialogue.speaker: Thug 2
dialogue.line: Mosquitoes?! You wanna get swatted, old timer?

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: WHOOSH

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: One thug lunged. Mr. Han barely moved, a blur of motion. Shiwoon blinked.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: THWACK

dialogue.speaker: Thug 1
dialogue.line: Ugh!

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The thug was suddenly on the ground, clutching his stomach. Mr. Han was standing exactly where he was before, hands in his pockets, a bored expression.

dialogue.speaker: Thug 3
dialogue.line: What the—?!

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: CRACK

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Another thug went down, seemingly tripping over his own feet, but Shiwoon could have sworn he saw Mr. Han's foot subtly extend and retract.

dialogue.speaker: Chun-woo Han
dialogue.line: Honestly, you kids need to learn some manners. And maybe some actual fighting skills.

dialogue.speaker: Thug 2
dialogue.line: (stuttering) Wh-what are you?!

dialogue.speaker: Chun-woo Han
dialogue.line: Just a teacher. Now, run along before I have to give you a pop quiz on pain.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: SCRAMBLE

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The remaining thugs scrambled away, leaving their unconscious comrades behind. Mr. Han glanced around, then continued his stroll as if nothing had happened.

dialogue.speaker: Shiwoon Yi
dialogue.line: (thought) What... what did I just see? Was that... real? He didn't even break a sweat. He just... moved. Like a ghost.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Shiwoon hurried home, his mind racing. The image of Mr. Han's effortless movements burned in his memory.

dialogue.speaker: Shiwoon Yi
dialogue.line: (thought) All my life, I've been pushed around. Kicked. Beaten. I've always wished I could be strong. To stand up for myself. To not be afraid.

dialogue.speaker: Shiwoon Yi
dialogue.line: (thought) That teacher... Mr. Han... he's different. He's not just strong, he's... something else. Could he... could he teach me?

dialogue.speaker: Shiwoon Yi
dialogue.line: (thought) No, that's crazy. He's just an English teacher. But... what if?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: A flicker of desperate hope ignited within Shiwoon, a spark in the darkness of his helplessness.

summary: Shiwoon Yi, a constantly bullied high school student, endures another day of torment. A mysterious and unconventional new English teacher, Chun-woo Han, arrives, later revealing a glimpse of incredible martial arts prowess to a shocked Shiwoon, igniting a desperate hope within him.
